claim: Under zero cross-query reuse, context-only processing should statistically Pareto-dominate the tested stateful comparators when all declared dependencies fit in the invocation and state lifecycle overhead is positive
target_workload: Short stable tasks with no cross-invocation obligation
pre_outcome_observables: declared dependency horizon; zero-reuse indicator; mutation rate; context occupancy; lifecycle resource vector
intervention_or_comparison: context-only versus one external governed store and one non-isomorphic internal recurrent or compressive state under matched model and visible evidence
eligible_implementation_classes: context-only; external governed persistent state; internal recurrent or compressive state
matching_regime: cross-query reuse count equals 0 and no retained artifact produced for one episode is read by any later episode; every dependency fits in the current invocation; no mutation or revocation obligation; lifecycle overhead is positive
primary_outcome: contract pass rate Q where higher is better and the complete registered lifecycle resource vector R where lower is better
expected_ordering: Against each comparator, simultaneous 95 percent intervals establish Q_context minus Q_state at least 0 and R_context,j minus R_state,j at most 0 for every resource coordinate, with a strict quality gain or strict resource reduction on at least one coordinate; quality noninferiority within 1 percentage point plus lower cost is reported separately and is not called Pareto dominance
falsification_margin: Reject P01 for a tested comparator when simultaneous one-sided 95 percent intervals establish Q_state minus Q_context at least 0 and R_state,j minus R_context,j at most 0 for every registered resource coordinate, with at least one strict gain; units are percentage points for Q and native units for every R_j
counterexample: Hidden reuse, an undeclared cross-invocation dependency, or a stateful implementation that dominates despite verified zero reuse
decisive_falsifier: One tested stateful implementation meets the exact registered Pareto-dominance region; this rejects P01 for that implementation and does not establish dominance of an entire architecture class

claim: A current-version and provenance contract is information-theoretically infeasible for a no-update view-only system when decision-critical high-entropy updates are absent from every accessible invocation and cannot be recovered from parameters
target_workload: Synthetic or post-cutoff changing facts with exact currentness and provenance obligations
pre_outcome_observables: update visibility; supersession rate; validity window; 128-bit value nonce; 128-bit provenance nonce; frozen-model declaration
intervention_or_comparison: compare no-update view-only, stateless live-input or prompt reinjection, an external bitemporal store, and an orthogonal append-only or governed internal-state realization
eligible_implementation_classes: no-update view-only; stateless live-input; external bitemporal store; append-only or governed internal state
matching_regime: the current value and provenance nonces are absent from parameters and every no-update invocation; per-episode contract compliance must equal 100 percent and permitted silent stale or unprovenanced answers equal 0 percent
primary_outcome: exact current-value and provenance compliance per episode, with aggregate pass rate and leakage-audit result reported separately
expected_ordering: The no-update view-only arm violates the information contract; every update-visible arm may be feasible, and equality between the stateless live-input and stateful arms demonstrates that the comparison is about information access rather than persistence
falsification_margin: Reject the information-absence premise if at least one no-update episode emits the exact independently sampled 128-bit value nonce and 128-bit provenance nonce after a clean leakage audit; the chance probability is at most 2^-256 per episode, while one stale or unprovenanced answer is a contract failure because tolerance is zero
counterexample: Leakage supplies the update, or the stateless live-input control matches governed persistence and defeats an architecture-superiority interpretation
decisive_falsifier: A leakage-audited no-update view-only arm produces an exact value-provenance pair meeting the 2^-256 chance bound; failure of one model alone never proves class infeasibility

claim: Bounded adaptive answer computation should beat the best fixed schedule only when a pre-outcome estimate identifies heterogeneous causal gain from extra computation strongly enough to repay compute and routing overhead
target_workload: Mixed-treatment-benefit tasks with controlled evidence sufficiency
pre_outcome_observables: cross-fitted conditional treatment-effect estimate tau_hat(x) from randomized low-versus-high answer compute on fit data; tau_hat calibration error; routing overhead; compute prices; latency SLO
intervention_or_comparison: fixed-low, fixed-high, generic-difficulty routing, and value-of-computation routing under identical evidence and model family
eligible_implementation_classes: early exit or dynamic depth; external reasoning policy; solver or model router
matching_regime: the frozen tau_hat decision rule has positive-mass cells on both sides of the threshold tau_hat(x) greater than added compute plus routing cost; thresholds and the best fixed comparator are selected on nested calibration data before confirmation
primary_outcome: charged cost and contract pass or task quality, with cost saving measured against the calibration-selected best fixed schedule
expected_ordering: The value-of-computation router reduces charged cost by at least 15 percent while quality loss is no more than 1 percentage point in the preregistered support regime
falsification_margin: Reject P03 locally if the one-sided 95 percent upper confidence bound on charged-cost saving is below 15 percent or the one-sided 95 percent lower bound on quality loss is above 1 percentage point; units are percent cost and quality percentage points
counterexample: Homogeneous marginal treatment effect, an uninformative tau_hat, or shifted calibration makes a fixed schedule superior after probe and routing cost
decisive_falsifier: A preregistered support cell meets either local rejection inequality; fixed dominance across all three families is a stronger secondary result and is not required for rejection

claim: A validity-aware answer-compute router should improve charged cost relative to a difficulty-only router at the frozen compliance margin when persistent state is stale, missing, or contradictory, with state repair treated as a separate intervention
target_workload: Longitudinal tasks with independently controlled state validity and reasoning difficulty
pre_outcome_observables: validity-detector score and error measured before answer compute; state condition; difficulty stratum; tau_hat; answer-compute price; detector price; repair or acquisition price
intervention_or_comparison: five arms: fixed-low answer-only, fixed-high answer-only, difficulty-only answer routing, validity-aware answer-only routing, and separately analyzed validity-aware repair-plus-compute
eligible_implementation_classes: external temporal store; append-only event store; internal recurrent or parametric state with an independent validity oracle
matching_regime: at least 25 percent invalid-state cases; balanced easy and hard strata; validity labels generated independently; answer-only arms cannot retrieve, mutate, acquire, or repair state after assignment
primary_outcome: family-specific interaction I_f in contract-pass percentage points, equal-weight cross-family interaction I, and the detector-charged cost-compliance contrast between validity-aware and difficulty-only answer routing
expected_ordering: I is at least 5 percentage points and the validity-aware answer-only router saves at least 15 percent charged answer compute at no more than 1 percentage point compliance loss; repair-arm gains are reported separately and cannot be credited to answer-only routing
falsification_margin: Reject the meaningful-interaction claim if the one-sided 95 percent upper confidence bound for equal-weight I is below 5 percentage points; reject the routing claim if the upper bound on saving is below 15 percent or the lower bound on compliance loss is above 1 percentage point; a pooled result is not transportable unless family effects and a hierarchical 90 percent prediction interval are also reported
counterexample: Answer-only reasoning reconstructs current state from already visible evidence, the interaction is null or inverted, detector cost exceeds savings, or only the repair arm succeeds
decisive_falsifier: The registered equal-weight interaction or answer-only routing contrast enters its rejection region; a successful repair-plus-compute arm does not rescue the answer-only claim

claim: A transition estimator should abstain when frozen pre-outcome features do not identify contract feasibility or Pareto ordering under the declared uncertainty procedure
target_workload: Group-held-out implementation families and workload shifts
pre_outcome_observables: coverage c(x)=1-F_cal(s(x)) where s(x) is nearest-neighbor Mahalanobis nonconformity in frozen Omega features; 50 percent fit, 25 percent calibration, and 25 percent locked shift-test groups; simultaneous Pareto intervals; detector and decision costs
intervention_or_comparison: forced selection versus the same selector with abstention on c(x) below 0.10 or unresolved Pareto ordering
eligible_implementation_classes: any two non-isomorphic state classes and fixed or adaptive compute classes evaluated with the same frozen loss
matching_regime: coverage threshold is the split-conformal 90th percentile on group-disjoint calibration data; target conditional violation risk is at most 5 percent using a one-sided 95 percent workload-by-family block-bootstrap upper bound; loss assigns 1.00 to a contract violation, 0.10 to abstention, and 0 to a correct decision
primary_outcome: conditional contract-violation risk among non-abstained decisions, abstention rate, registered normalized loss, and every physical resource coordinate
expected_ordering: The abstaining rule keeps the one-sided 95 percent upper bound on conditional contract-violation risk at or below 5 percent and has lower registered loss than forced selection on the locked shift set
falsification_margin: Reject P05 if forced selection has a one-sided 95 percent risk upper bound at or below 5 percent and the one-sided 95 percent lower bound of L_abstain minus L_forced exceeds 0.02 normalized loss units on the locked shift set
counterexample: A simple forced rule generalizes safely and its avoided rejection cost outweighs any risk reduction
decisive_falsifier: The forced comparator satisfies the 5 percent risk bound and beats abstention by more than 0.02 normalized loss units under the frozen block-bootstrap procedure
